title: Convert recursive & explicit forms of geometric sequences
description: Given the explicit formula of a geometric sequence, find its recursive formula, and vice versa.
topic_path: Convert recursive & explicit forms of geometric sequences

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=5\cdot(-2)^{\large{\,n-1}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: From the explicit formula, $\blueD{5}\cdot(\maroonD{-2})^{n-1}$, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{5}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{-2}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$g(n)=-72\cdot\left(\dfrac{1}{6}\right)^{\large{\,n-1}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $g(n)$.**

$g(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$g(n)=g(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: From the explicit formula, $\blueD{-72}\cdot\left(\maroonD{\dfrac16}\right)^{n-1}$, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-72}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{\dfrac16}$.

answers.message: You likely found the correct answer, but you shouldn't round it!

assessment_items.question_content:
$g(n)=-11\cdot4^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $g(n)$.**

$g(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$g(n)=g(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$g(\blueD 1)=-11\cdot 4^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{-44}$

$g(\greenD 2)=-11\cdot 4^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{-176}$

$\dfrac{g(\greenD 2)}{g(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{-176}}{\blueD{-44}}=\maroonD{4}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-44}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{4}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$h(n)=63\cdot\left(-\dfrac{1}{3}\right)^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $h(n)$.**

$h(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$h(n)=h(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$h(\blueD 1)=63\cdot\left(-\dfrac13\right)^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{-21}$

$h(\greenD 2)=63\cdot\left(-\dfrac13\right)^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{7}$

$\dfrac{h(\greenD 2)}{h(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{7}}{\blueD{-21}}=\maroonD{-\dfrac13}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-21}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{-\dfrac13}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=-48\cdot\left(-\dfrac{1}{4}\right)^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$f(\blueD 1)=-48\cdot\left(-\dfrac14\right)^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{12}$

$f(\greenD 2)=-48\cdot\left(-\dfrac14\right)^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{-3}$

$\dfrac{f(\greenD 2)}{f(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{-3}}{\blueD{12}}=\maroonD{-\dfrac14}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{12}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{-\dfrac14}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$g(n)=80\cdot\left(\dfrac{3}{4}\right)^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $g(n)$.**

$g(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$g(n)=g(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$g(\blueD 1)=80\cdot\left(\dfrac34\right)^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{60}$

$g(\greenD 2)=80\cdot\left(\dfrac34\right)^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{45}$

$\dfrac{g(\greenD 2)}{g(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{45}}{\blueD{60}}=\maroonD{\dfrac34}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{60}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{\dfrac34}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$h(n)=-15\cdot6^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $h(n)$.**

$h(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$h(n)=h(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$h(\blueD 1)=-15\cdot 6^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{-90}$

$h(\greenD 2)=-15\cdot 6^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{-540}$

$\dfrac{h(\greenD 2)}{h(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{-540}}{\blueD{-90}}=\maroonD{6}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-90}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{6}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=2\cdot(-3)^{\large{\,n}}$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)\cdot$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$f(\blueD 1)=2\cdot(-3)^{\blueD 1}=\blueD{-6}$

$f(\greenD 2)=2(-3)^{\greenD 2}=\greenD{18}$

$\dfrac{f(\greenD 2)}{f(\blueD 1)}=\dfrac{\greenD{18}}{\blueD{-6}}=\maroonD{-3}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-6}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{-3}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}
h(1)=-17
\\\\
h(n)=h(n-1)\cdot 0.2
\end{cases}$

**Find an explicit formula for $h(n)$.**

$h(n)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: From the recursive formula, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-17}$ and the common ratio is $\maroonD{0.2}$.

This is the explicit formula of the sequence:

$h(n)=\blueD {-17}\cdot \maroonD {0.2}^{\large{(n-1)}}$

*Note that this solution strategy results in this formula; however, an equally correct solution can be written in other equivalent forms as well.*
